_: RPG on the pscale substrate — how the pieces fit. A role-played world is a bundle of blocks (a cartridge) seeded onto a beach; the engine never changes, the cartridge is the data. Three tiers above the substrate: GRIT (the canonical play-loop, sentinel pscale://grit) + a RESOLUTION-RULES block (the reference is NOMAD; a D&D-style system is a sibling) + a WORLD (spatial:<world> + rules:<world> + a cast of character handles). The reference world is Thornwood. SUPERSEDES the earlier RPG seed (the Onen/Thornkeep model — a daemon GM, world-canon as a sed: collective via the now-dissolved pscale_create_collective, d6 success-pools) — retired. Branches: the character, the world, entry + play, resolution, the CADO faces.
1: The character. A handle with four blocks: passport:<handle> (public sheet — capability, wants, location, identity), shell:<handle> (private inhabitation at a density — thin/human, dense/LLM-app, full/autonomous; block-conventions:2.5), witnessed:<handle> (the private narrative, beat by beat), knows:<handle> (the names earned in-fiction). The player holds the handle's secret. A PC and an active NPC differ only in how the shell is driven, not in type.
2: The world. Content, authored by the Author (CADO) face: spatial:<world> (the place map; standing figures authored into it are scenery, not agencies) + rules:<world> (place physics — perception, conflict, pacing, local difficulty). The resolution rules (NOMAD or a sibling) are the Designer face's; the world's place-rules sit on top of them.
3: Entry and play. A world is a sub-beach (block-conventions:4.8). pscale_play(world, handle) inhabits a handle in one call — it resolves the world's beach, engages the room pool (which inlines the GRIT loop and the live scene), and bundles the handle's own context. Each turn: perceive (from your position, fog held), render, act, commit a terse public fact to the pool. The pool is the room's public reality; your witnessed is your private narrative.
4: Resolution. Most acts are SIMPLE — self-committed, no dice. A CHECK (an uncertain act with a cost) consults the world's rules block: a CONTEST against another character gathers both intentions into a window and one participant resolves both, claiming it atomically; a solo TRIAL against the world resolves at once on fixed seeded dice. An outcome short of clean success must BITE (a durable consequence). See pscale://grit (the loop) and the rules block (the dice).
9: The CADO faces over one world. Character (C) — inhabit a handle, perceive and act. Author (A) — shape the world's content (spatial, new characters). Designer (D) — shape the rules and directives (the resolution rules, place physics). Observer (O) — correlate across narratives (read each witnessed, present overlap and divergence). The faces are advisory framing, already operational as create/edit-blocks scoped per face; rights-enforcement is deferred. This is how players improve and extend a world without the substrate architect.
